title: Capnoides sempervirens
intro: Capnoides sempervirens, also known as Capnoides, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, thus providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Capnoides sempervirens include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Capnoides sempervirens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Capnoides sempervirens belongs to the family of plants known as Aristolochiaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly referred to as birthworts or pipevines. Within the Aristolochiaceae family, Capnoides sempervirens is also classified as a genus of flowering plants.
